research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 Events and Alerts resources (media type 2023-01-01)

official_sources.title: Return Events from One Project | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/events; query parameters eventType (array), clusterNames (array), pageNum (default 1), itemsPerPage (default 100, max 500), includeCount (default true); response {results, links, totalCount}; project read-only role; groups and projects are synonymous

official_sources.title: MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: listGroupEvents params minDate/maxDate are format:date-time (ISO 8601), eventType and excludedEventType are arrays, includeRaw defaults false; response media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json (GroupPaginatedEventView); event views require created/eventTypeName/id and carry optional username/remoteAddress/raw; listGroupAlerts declares query param status with enum OPEN|TRACKING|CLOSED, response PaginatedAlertView; alert views require alertConfigId/created/eventTypeName/id/status/updated with status values OPEN/TRACKING/CLOSED/CANCELLED; ApiError is served as application/json

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: pagination link relations self (always) / next (omitted on the last page) / previous (omitted on the first); links live in the response body; an empty list is 200 with results: [] while a nonexistent context is 404; error envelope {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} with the RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND example

official_sources.title: Versioned Atlas Administration API Overview - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: Accept: application/vnd.atlas.<yyyy-mm-dd>+json dated media-type format and 406 semantics for wrong/missing version dates

research.language_rationale: The audit tooling this exporter feeds is a JVM shop. MongoDB publishes no official Java SDK for the Administration API (the Java driver is a data-plane client), so java.net.http.HttpClient against the documented versioned REST surface is the credible zero-dependency integration path.

verified_facts: Project events are read via GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/events with dated media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json and Bearer auth.
eventType is an array query parameter (repeat eventType=<VALUE> per requested type); minDate/maxDate are ISO 8601 date-times, so their colons percent-encode as %3A in a query string.
List responses carry {results, links, totalCount}; links rel values are self (always), next (omitted on the last page), previous (omitted on the first); clients page by following the rel=next href.

An empty collection returns 200 with results: [] — it is not an error; a nonexistent context returns 404 with the application/json ApiError envelope {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} (example errorCode RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND).
Event documents require id, eventTypeName and created (ISO 8601 UTC); username is optional and absent for system events; id is the natural deduplication key when pages drift while new events arrive.

Project alerts are read via GET /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/alerts; the status filter accepts exactly OPEN, TRACKING or CLOSED, and alert documents require id, alertConfigId, eventTypeName, status, created, updated.
pageNum defaults to 1, itemsPerPage defaults to 100 (max 500), includeCount defaults to true.
